examples.title: Basic Egrep Searches
examples.description: Common egrep command examples for text searching
examples.code:
# Basic pattern matching
egrep 'error' logfile.txt

# Case-insensitive search
egrep -i 'ERROR' logfile.txt

# Show line numbers
egrep -n 'warning' logfile.txt

# Count matching lines
egrep -c 'success' logfile.txt

# Invert match (show non-matching lines)
egrep -v 'debug' logfile.txt

# Whole word matching
egrep -w 'function' code.c

# Multiple patterns (OR)
egrep 'error|warning|fatal' logfile.txt
examples.explanation: These are fundamental egrep commands for various text searching scenarios in Unix/Linux systems.

examples.title: Extended Regex Patterns
examples.description: Examples using extended metacharacters in egrep
examples.code:
# Using + (one or more)
egrep '[0-9]+' numbers.txt

# Using ? (zero or one)
egrep 'colour?r' colors.txt

# Using alternation |
egrep 'cat|dog|bird' pets.txt

# Using grouping and quantifiers
egrep '(ab)+' test.txt

egrep 'a{3}' repeated.txt

# Using character classes
egrep '[A-Z]{3}' uppercase.txt

# Using anchors
egrep '^ERROR' logfile.txt

egrep 'finished$' logfile.txt

# Empty line detection
egrep '^$' empty.txt
examples.explanation: Demonstrates extended regex metacharacters (+, ?, |, (), {}, anchors) in practical egrep patterns.

examples.title: Complex Pattern Examples
examples.description: Real-world egrep patterns for validation and extraction
examples.code:
# Match phone numbers (XXX-XXX-XXXX)
egrep '[0-9]{3}-[0-9]{3}-[0-9]{4}' contacts.txt

# Match email addresses
egrep '[a-zA-Z0-9._%]+@[a-zA-Z0-9.]+\.[a-z]{2,}' emails.txt

# Match IP addresses
egrep '[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}\.[0-9]{1,3}' network.txt

# Match dates (YYYY-MM-DD)
egrep '[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}' dates.txt

# Match URLs
egrep 'https?://[a-zA-Z0-9.-]+' webpage.html

# Find repeated words
egrep '\b(\w+)\s+\1\b' text.txt

# Match lines with specific starting pattern
egrep '^\[ERROR\]' logfile.txt

# Match blank or empty lines
egrep '^\s*$' text.txt
examples.explanation: These complex patterns demonstrate practical applications of egrep for data validation and extraction in real scenarios.

examples.title: File and Directory Searching
examples.description: Using egrep with files and recursive search
examples.code:
# Search in multiple files
egrep 'pattern' file1.txt file2.txt file3.txt

# Search all files in current directory
egrep 'pattern' *

# Recursive search in directories
egrep -r 'function' /home/user/project/

# Show only filenames with matches
egrep -l 'error' *.log

# Show only filenames without matches
egrep -L 'error' *.log

# Combine options
egrep -rn 'TODO' --include='*.py' project/

# Search in compressed files (zgrep)
zgrep 'pattern' file.gz

# Use grep -E (same as egrep)
grep -E 'pattern' file.txt
examples.explanation: Advanced egrep usage for searching across multiple files and directories in Unix/Linux systems.
